mnemonics: COCA Kills Attacks: Ciphertext-Only, Known-Plaintext, Chosen-Plaintext, Adaptive-Chosen-Plaintext, Chosen-Ciphertext, Key-Manipulation, and Side-Channel attacks are the main types to remember.
keyPoints: Cryptanalysis is the study of methods to breach cryptographic security systems.
Attacks are classified based on the information available to the cryptanalyst (e.g., ciphertext-only, known-plaintext).
The goal is to find weaknesses in cryptographic algorithms or their implementation to recover the secret key or plaintext.
commonMistakes: Confusing cryptanalysis (breaking codes) with cryptography (creating codes).
Assuming all attacks require vast amounts of data; some side-channel attacks can succeed with minimal information.
Overlooking implementation flaws and focusing solely on theoretical algorithmic weaknesses.
